refnamediv.refpurpose: keyword to check function arguments. Arguments block must be the expression in the function.

refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.programlisting: arguments argName1 (dimensions) datatype {validators} = defaultValue ... argNameN end

listitem.para: Name of the arguments from function protoptype.
All input arguments must be declared in arguments block and in the same order.
Expected (i, j, ...) dimensions for an input argument like (2, 3, 4). Must be a list of integer values or : or input variable name.
Matrix form (4, [3 6 10]) can be used to except size of 4 x 3 or 4 x 6 or 4 x 10.
If input argument is a scalar and expected dimensions are bigger, input argument will be expand to match the expected dimensions.
If expected dimensions is a vector (1xn or nx1) and input argument is a vector, input will be transposed to match expected form.
Expected datatype of input arguments. Inputs must be of datatype or convertable to this type.
datatype can be double, (u)int(8/16/32/64), boolean, string.
Comma-separated list of predefined functions used to check input validity. Each validator must succeed for the input to be accepted.
Two equivalent syntaxes are accepted inside the braces:
Short form — only the validator name, the checked argument is implicit. Available for validators that take only the checked value as parameter (e.g. mustBeReal, mustBePositive, mustBeFinite, ...).

Function-call form — the checked argument is passed explicitly. Required for validators that take additional parameters (e.g. mustBeGreaterThan, mustBeInRange, mustBeMember, mustBeA, ...). Also valid for single-argument validators.
If an input is not given by caller, variable will take this value.
All variables with default value must be at the end of the prototype.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.programlisting:
function test(x, y) arguments x (1, 10) double {mustBeReal, mustBePositive} y double = 1 end
disp(x, y) end
//rejected test() //x is required test(a, list(1)) // a list cannot be convert to a double test(0:9) // x must be > 0
